title: Fix broken button/border corners across shared components
description:
The 1.5px green outline on the ghost buttons renders with broken/notched corners — the border shows square corners poking past the rounded clip. User first reported the home screen 'Add a plant' and 'Check in' buttons; then the same defect on the 'Add a new room' button in the 'Where do they live?' add-plant sheet. Asked for a general check-in on ALL shared components to make sure every corner renders correctly.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed): the anti-pattern .border(color, width:) followed by .cornerRadius(r). SwiftUI's .border draws a rectangular (square-cornered) stroke; the later .cornerRadius() clips the view to a rounded shape, which slices off the straight border at each corner — leaving the corners looking wrong. The fix is to stroke a rounded shape instead, e.g. .overlay(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: r, style: .continuous).stroke(color, lineWidth: width)) (and clip any background separately), rather than .border(...).cornerRadius(...).

BOTH reported cases come from ONE shared component: SproutGhostButtonStyle in Sources/DesignSystem/Buttons.swift:24 (the offending lines are .border(...) at 33-36 then .cornerRadius(18) at 37). It's used in Sources/Views/HomeView.swift:156 and :169 (Add a plant / Check in) and Sources/Views/AddFlowView.swift:194 (Add a new room) — so fixing the style fixes all three usages at once.

GENERAL CHECK-IN RESULT: grepping Sources/ for '.border(' finds only TWO occurrences — SproutGhostButtonStyle (above) and one more one-off at Sources/Views/PlantEditView.swift:165 (same .border(...).cornerRadius(...) pattern, likely the same visual bug on the Edit Plant form). No other shared component uses .border; the other button styles (SproutCreamButtonStyle, primary button) and cards/chips already use .cornerRadius on a filled background or an .overlay(RoundedRectangle...) stroke, so they're not affected. So the corner-rendering sweep is small: fix SproutGhostButtonStyle once, plus the PlantEditView:165 one-off, and both reported symptoms plus any latent one are resolved.

Why it matters: it's a visible polish defect on primary CTAs (home add/check-in) and in the onboarding add-plant flow — cheap, contained fix with outsized visual payoff.

title: Expose sun/humidity levers for preset rooms in Add Room
description:
When adding a new room via the Add Room sheet (Sources/Views/AddRoomView.swift), a preset room type (Living Room, Kitchen, Bedroom, Bathroom, etc.) only shows a read-only 'TYPICAL SETTINGS' card with two static chips — Brightness (e.g. Bright) and Humidity (e.g. Normal) — with NO way to adjust them. The editable Direct Sun / Indirect Sun / Humidity segmented controls only appear when you pick 'Other…' (the CUSTOM ROOM path). User's point: rooms of the same type vary a lot — we don't know what every living room looks like (some are brighter/darker/more humid than others) — so the direct-sun / indirect-sun / humidity levers should be available on EVERY room-type option, with the chosen preset simply setting them as editable DEFAULTS that the user can then override before saving.

CONTEXT / CODE ANCHORS (the machinery already exists — this is mostly surfacing it):
- AddRoomView already stores @State directSun / indirectSun / humidity (Sources/Views/AddRoomView.swift:17-19) and already ADOPTS the selected preset's defaults into that state when a preset is chosen (adoptPresetDefaults, ~lines 133-139: directSun = preset.directSun; indirectSun = preset.indirectSun; humidity = preset.humidity). So Save already uses the preset's values even today.
- The branch that hides them is ~lines 101-105: for a preset it renders presetSummarySection(preset) — the read-only TYPICAL SETTINGS chips (~line 144) — and only the 'Other' path shows the editable segmented controls.
- The exact editable segmented lever UI (Direct Sun Low/Medium/High, Indirect Sun Low/Medium/High, Humidity Dry/Normal/Moist, each with an ⓘ info popover) already exists in two places to reuse: the 'Other' custom path in AddRoomView, and RoomEditorView (Sources/Views/RoomsView.swift:121) which the EDIT-room flow uses. So the same controls that edit an existing room just need to show on the preset path too.
- Preset default values come from RoomPreset.common (Sources/Model/RoomPreset.swift); Brightness is inferred from directSun+indirectSun (Brightness.inferred), and the schedule factor from RoomEnvironment.factor(brightness,humidity).

So the essence: on the preset path, pre-fill the three levers from the preset and show the SAME editable segmented controls (instead of / in addition to the read-only TYPICAL SETTINGS chips), so a user can fine-tune before saving rather than having to save then go edit the room afterward. (Related: the current footer copy even says 'fine-tune it later by editing the room' — this idea lets them fine-tune up front.)

Why it matters: preset light/humidity are guesses; letting the user correct them at creation makes the watering schedule accurate from day one instead of requiring a follow-up edit.
